title: Graphic Designer for DTF T-Shirt Printing
brief: I'm looking for an experienced graphic designer to recreate and refine an existing illustration for DTF (Direct to Film) t-shirt printing. I have two versions of the design — one generated by AI with strong neon detail, smoke effects and lighting, and one I attempted to recreate manually. I need someone to combine the best elements of both into one clean, polished, print-ready file. The goal is the neon-style lips and hand from the AI version paired with the gradient smoke effect from my manual version, using these exact colours: FFB339, FF3B58 and F714FF. Everything sits on a fully transparent background and needs to be optimised for DTF printing. The final result should feel bold and refined — not a rough recreation but a genuinely improved version that builds on both references. On the technical side, here is what I need delivered: Transparent background, clean edges suitable for direct film transfer High resolution minimum 300 DPI Final files in PNG plus the original editable source file For the designer, I am looking for someone with strong illustration experience, comfortable working from reference images and matching a specific style. Experience with DTF, screen print or apparel print file preparation is preferred — please share examples of similar work when you apply. I'll be sending an image of the design once I communicate with the designer.
